original:
Is that a twitch of impatience on the impassive face of the Excubitor Orbis? But truly, the read is in his clerk; she almost cringes at the insufficiency of your answer.

Oak lets out a slow breath.

"So be it," he says, looking away as if you're no longer worth his time.

The Excubitor Orbis takes in your comment, his face impassive as the stone this hangar was blasted from.

His clerk's eyes are downcast.

Is that a twitch of impatience on the impassive face of the Excubitor Orbis? But truly, the read is in his clerk; she almost cringes at the insufficiency of your answer.

Oak lets out a slow breath, and looks away from you as if your words are worth nothing to him. He makes a subtle movement with his hand.

Is that a twitch of impatience on the impassive face of the Excubitor Orbis? But truly, the read is in his clerk; she almost cringes at the insufficiency of your answer.

Oak lets out a slow breath. "So be it," he says, looking away, as if you're not worth his time.

A quartet of armored Knights take this as their cue to approach Bornanew's coffin. Together, without needing a signal, they lift as one and bear him toward the door at the far end of the hangar. Their silhouettes make long shadows in the wedge of warm light.

The stony gaze of Gideon Oak then turns slowly toward the prisoner in a simple e-suit and bound by hand-restraints - a bit of symbolism more than security measure  - flanked by your guards.

"Keepfaith," he says.

She inhales as if she might speak, some righteous outburst, but falters. Her eyes fall toward the ground.

The stony gaze of Gideon Oak turns slowly toward the prisoner in a simple temp suit with hand-restraints - a bit of symbolism more than security measure  - flanked by your guards.

"Keepfaith," he says.

She inhales as if she might speak, some righteous outburst, but falters. Her eyes fall toward the ground.

Oak lets out a slow breath.

He makes a subtle movement with his hand, and a pair of the huge, armored guards step to Keepfaith.

Faceless in their armor, they take her away.

You are left with Oak, his clerk, and Bornanew.

"The glove," says the Excubitor Orbis.
